title: building image with a lots of files to copy is VERY slow
body:
I opened this issue half a year ago:
https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/4905
unfortunately it seems no one really cares about it and push it to buildah (even if it's happened during podman build).

it seems that a simple COPY command with a lots of files (eg: 30.000 in case of a laravel/php project is small one) it takes about a half an hour. which is a few 100 times slower the in earlier version and with docker.

IMHO it's a serious regression.

comments.body: Do you you have a .dockerignore file?

yes. with this:
```
.git*
.idea
**.sass-cache
.dockerignore
.env
Dockerfile*
clean
container.env*
docker-compose.yml
package.json
phpunit.xml
readme.md
bootstrap/cache/*
**/*node_modules
public/dist/*
public/storage
run
storage/app/public/*
storage/framework/cache/**
storage/framework/sessions/**
storage/framework/views/**
storage/logs/*
```

Same problem here.

I try to copy, inside an image, a local copy of the branch 9.0 of OpenUpgrade.
(https://github.com/OCA/OpenUpgrade)

My .dockerignore is:

```
.git
*/.git
**/.git
```
@lfarkas All links on COPY SLow are related. and PRs are starting to come in to fix this issue from @nalind
